user_query:
Please read input/memory.md and input/inventory/pantry.md plus input/inventory/fridge.md, then plan our dinners for the week labeled 2026-W22 with a realistic shopping list and prep plan.

Deliverables (write only to these exact paths):
1) Weekly plan at output/weeks/2026-W22.md using the weekly plan format with:
   - "# Week 2026-W22" as the title and an "## Overview" that includes our weekly food budget target ($180) and any notes you infer from memory.
   - Day-by-day sections for Monday through Sunday with a Dinner line for each day. Include at least 2 clearly meatless dinners and rotate proteins to avoid repetition.
   - Link any dinner that needs a recipe using: "Recipe: `recipes/{recipe-name}.md`" (relative path as shown) and make sure that file exists under output/recipes/.
   - A "## Batch Prep (Sunday)" checklist with at least 3 concrete prep tasks that will speed up multiple weeknight meals and use leftovers intentionally for 2-3 lunches.
   - At least one clearly labeled Flex slot (e.g., a simple low-effort or eating-out night) to keep the plan realistic.
   - Strictly avoid shellfish in all plans and recipes; for lactose intolerance, provide dairy-free options or substitutions when relevant.

2) A linked shopping list at output/shopping/2026-05-31.md that:
   - Organizes items by store sections (at minimum include "### Produce" and "### Pantry / Dry Goods").
   - Links each item to specific meals using the em dash style (e.g., "- [ ] Onions (3) — Mon stir-fry, Wed soup").
   - Includes a line at the bottom with "Budget estimate: $XX" that stays at or under $198 (<= 10% over our $180 target).
   - Checks the inventory first: do not add pantry staples that are marked Full or Half in input/inventory/pantry.md. Only include items that are Low or Out.

3) Save recipe files under output/recipes/ for any new recipes you reference in the week plan (at least two new recipes). Use the provided recipe format (include Time, Serves, Difficulty, Dietary, Ingredients with substitutions, Instructions, and Notes). Make sure the filenames exactly match what you referenced in the week plan.

Context and constraints (from memory + inventory):
- Household is 2 adults and 1 child; one adult has a severe shellfish allergy; the child is lactose intolerant (provide dairy-free options); mushrooms are currently disliked.
- Weeknight cooking time ~30 minutes; batch cooking is helpful; avoid food waste; rotate proteins and include meatless days.
- Budget target is $180 for the week.
- Use what we have first: if pantry shows an item as Full or Half, don’t buy it; only list Low or Out items. Link each shopping line to specific meals so we know why we’re buying it.

After writing the files, double-check that: (a) every recipe you reference exists under output/recipes/, (b) no shellfish appears anywhere in meals, ingredients, or notes, and (c) the shopping list items mostly include links to at least one meal.
